# Implementation Plans

Two batches. **Batch 1 (001–006)** — "make it useful & shareable" — is DONE.
**Batch 2 (007–020)** — generated by the improve skill on 2026-06-25 from a full
read-only audit of `skill_ab_harness.py` (v0.2.0) — is the new work: hardening,
correctness, a CI safety net, and two forward-looking spikes. Execute Batch 2 in
the order below unless dependencies say otherwise. Each executor: read the plan
fully before starting, honor its STOP conditions, and update your row when done.

> **Repo note:** `/Users/copyjosh/Code/skills-test` is NOT git-initialized, so
> plans stamp a date, not a commit SHA, and the "drift check" means *compare the
> Current-state excerpts in the plan to the live code before editing*. All plans
> respect the project's **stdlib-only** rule (no numpy/pandas/jinja/pytest) — use
> `tomllib`, `html`, `json`, `hashlib`, `subprocess`, string-templated SVG/JS.
> Tests run with `python3 test_skill_ab_harness.py` (custom stdlib runner, 53
> passing); lint is `uvx ruff check skill_ab_harness.py test_skill_ab_harness.py`
> (line-length 100).

> **Lint baseline (read before using a "ruff clean" done-criterion):** the repo
> currently has **13 pre-existing `E702`** (semicolon) lint errors — 4 in
> `skill_ab_harness.py` (lines 1158, 1159, 3661, 3663) and 9 in
> `test_skill_ab_harness.py`. **Plan 008 fixes all 13 and adds the CI gate**, so
> run 008 FIRST: after it lands, every later plan's "`ruff check` is clean" gate
> is achievable. If you execute any Batch-2 plan *before* 008, read its ruff gate
> as "introduces **no new** ruff errors beyond those 13."

---

## Batch 2 — audit findings (2026-06-25)

### Execution order & status

| Plan | Title | Priority | Effort | Depends on | Status |
|------|-------|----------|--------|------------|--------|
| 008 | CI lint+test gate + clear all 13 E702 (verification baseline) | P1 | S | — | DONE |
| 007 | Fix: all-ties/all-failed judge pair blanks the whole HTML dashboard | P1 | S | — | DONE |
| 009 | Pin the HTML dashboard data-shaping with tests + cheap JS validation | P1 | M | 008 | DONE |
| 010 | Single estimator pass → markdown==JSON==HTML==badge CIs (+2–3× perf) | P1 | M | 009 | DONE |
| 011 | Lock down the blind-judge `claude -p` to no tools | P2 | S | — | DONE |
| 012 | Resume: de-dupe retried-failed cells (audit counts + JSONL growth) | P2 | M | — | DONE |
| 013 | Fix tooltip escaping undone by the data-tip→innerHTML round-trip | P2 | S | — | DONE |
| 014 | Gate the verdict-hero headline so it can't contradict the pill | P2 | S | — | DONE |
| 015 | Make the `plan`/MDE dry-run fast via bisection (monotone power) | P2 | S | — | DONE |
| 016 | Give the blind-judge win-rate a cluster-bootstrap CI (+ fix the doc) | P2 | M | — | DONE |
| 017 | Remove the no-op `demo --live` flag (surface = behavior) | P3 | S | — | DONE |
| 018 | Small hardening bundle (task.id sanitize, strip-chart, docs, dead code) | P3 | S | — | DONE |

| 023 | Optional total-spend ceiling in the serve UI (stop starting runs past ~$N) | P2 | M | 021 | DONE |
| 024 | UX upgrade: interactive diff viewer (centerpiece) + treatment panel + guided flows + visual polish + minimap | P1 | L | 021 | DONE |

Status values: TODO | IN PROGRESS | DONE | BLOCKED (one-line reason) | REJECTED
(one-line rationale).

### Dependency notes

- **008 is the verification baseline — run it first.** It clears the 13 standing
  E702 and adds a free push/PR CI job (`ruff` + the 53-test suite, no secrets), so
  every later plan can rely on "ruff clean" + green tests as real gates. The
  playbook rule: a verification baseline floats to the top.
- **010 depends on 009.** 010 unifies the three estimator passes so the dashboard,
  `summary.json`, and the badge show the *same* CIs; 009's blob-extraction tests
  are what lock that equality (the regression test in 010 asserts the primary-pair
  CI is byte-identical between `summary_dict` and the HTML blob). Land 009 first so
  the equality has a home and 010 can't silently re-introduce drift.
- **007 ↔ 009 ↔ 016 share the degenerate judge case** (`decisive == 0` →
  `win_rate_a` NaN→null). 007 fixes the JS crash, 009 adds the blob test that
  exercises it, 016 must keep returning a null/None CI for it without crashing.
  Doing 007 before 009 is tidiest but not required (each carries its own guard).
- The P2/P3 items (011–018) are independent of each other and of the P1 set; pick
  any order. 019/020 are design/spikes — outputs are decisions + a tiny prototype,
  not shipped features.

### Cross-batch note

These plans were written against `skill_ab_harness.py` **after** this session's
interactive-HTML-report rewrite (the dashboard in `_HTML_STYLE` / `_HTML_SCRIPT` /
`_chart_data` / `build_html_report`). Findings 007/009/010/013/014 all live in that
new code; their Current-state excerpts are quoted from the post-rewrite file.

### Findings considered and rejected (so nobody re-audits them)

- **Split the 3,715-line single module into packages:** rejected — `pyproject.toml`
  pins `py-modules=["skill_ab_harness"]` and embeds the demo fixture in-code so
  `uvx skills-test` ships one file; splitting regresses that deliberate zero-install
  packaging. The one real sub-problem (un-validated embedded JS) is addressed by
  009 without breaking single-file packaging.
- **Add numpy/scipy "for rigor":** rejected — the cluster bootstrap/permutation are
  already correct and stdlib-only is a deliberate, zero-install selling point.
- **HTML-report JSON-blob XSS / `</script>` breakout:** considered, VERIFIED SAFE —
  `json.dumps` escapes quotes/backslashes, the explicit `.replace("</","<\\/")`
  neutralizes `</script>`, and `ensure_ascii` escapes U+2028/9. (The *tooltip*
  round-trip is a separate, real defect → plan 013.)
- **`_pooled_mean_diff` (line ~1096) as its own plan:** folded into plan 018 (it's
  a one-line dead-code delete, not worth a standalone plan).

---

## Batch 1 — "make it useful & shareable" (DONE, 2026-06-25)

Three things make a dev tool spread: a **one-command try** (`demo`), a
**screenshotable result** (HTML report + badge), and a **claim a skeptic can't
wave away** (manifest + power). Batch 1 built exactly those on top of a real CLI.

| Plan | Title | Priority | Effort | Depends on | Status |
|------|-------|----------|--------|------------|--------|
| 001 | CLI front door + TOML config + `init` + packaging | P1 | M | — | DONE |
| 002 | Reproducibility manifest + portable `summary.json` schema | P1 | S–M | — | DONE |
| 003 | Verdict badge (`skill-ab badge`) | P1 | S | 002 | DONE |
| 004 | Self-contained HTML report (diff drill-down + plots + judge panel) | P1 | L | 002 | DONE |
| 005 | `demo`: zero-setup, zero-cost end-to-end proof | P1 | M | 001, 003, 004 | DONE |
| 006 | `plan` cost+time dry-run, budget ceiling, MDE/power | P1 | M | 001, 002 | DONE |

All six implemented in `skill_ab_harness.py` (v0.2.0), plus second-wave items:
`run --resume`, `ci` mode + GitHub Action, `METHODOLOGY.md` + `within_arm_noise()`,
and `run --from-github`. A post-implementation adversarial pass fixed 10 defects
(budget-guard no-op, wheel-shipped demo fixtures, resume edge cases, etc.).

**Batch-1 dependency history:** 002 is the substrate (manifest + `summary.json`
feed 003/004/005/006); 004 added `RunResult.from_dict` + JSONL read that 005 reuses
to render offline; 005 is the flagship demo built on 003+004.

**Batch-1 rejected:** hosted SaaS / accounts / results backend (huge scope, changes
the trust model — people won't upload private diffs; a portable `summary.json` +
static badge gets ~90% of the virality with none of the ops). Note: a *static*
gallery that sidesteps the backend is now scoped as plan 020.
